skill.name: firebase-development-project-setup
skill.description: Guides initialization of new Firebase projects with CLI setup, architecture choices, emulators, TypeScript functions, rules, tests, and first commit workflow.
skill.summary: Initialize Firebase projects with a structured setup workflow and emulator-first defaults.
skill.author: 2389-research

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many Ruby backtick and weak crypto hits, but review found these are Markdown inline code and command examples, not executable code. The real risk is operational: the skill instructs agents to run Firebase, git, and npm commands and to create environment files, so use should require normal user confirmation and workspace awareness. No prompt injection, credential exfiltration, or malicious remote network behavior was found in SKILL.md.

medium_findings.title: Agent-Guided External CLI Execution
medium_findings.description: The static Ruby backtick findings are false positives from Markdown code formatting, but the skill does guide execution of Firebase, git, npm, and shell commands. These commands are legitimate for Firebase setup, yet they can change repositories, install packages, authenticate Firebase CLI sessions, and start local services.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file contains explicit setup commands, although they are not hidden or malicious. The risk is moderate because execution affects the local workspace and Firebase authentication state.

medium_findings.title: Environment File Creation Workflow
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs creation of .env.example, .env, and hosting/.env.local files while also recommending .gitignore entries. This is a legitimate Firebase setup pattern, but users could accidentally place secrets in local files or commit them if safeguards fail.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The instructions directly mention environment files and gitignore protection. There is no evidence of reading or exfiltrating secrets, so the concern is handling risk rather than malicious access.

low_findings.title: Loopback Emulator URL Flagged as Network Risk
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URL and IP findings point to http://127.0.0.1:4000 for the Firebase Emulator UI. This is a local loopback endpoint used for development, not a remote data exfiltration target.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Both network findings reference localhost emulator access. The semantic context is Firebase development verification, not external network communication.

low_findings.title: Weak Crypto and Network Reconnaissance Heuristics Are Not Supported
low_findings.description: The reported weak cryptographic algorithm and network reconnaissance findings appear to match harmless wording in the description and authentication section. No cryptographic implementation, scanning command, or reconnaissance workflow was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines describe Firebase project setup and authentication choices. They do not include weak algorithms, cryptographic code, or host discovery instructions.

dangerous_patterns.title: Workspace-Modifying Setup Commands
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill includes commands that create directories, initialize git, run Firebase init, install dependencies, run tests, and start emulators. These are expected for the skill, but they require a trusted workspace and user approval before execution.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The commands are visible and directly tied to the setup workflow. They are not malicious, but they can make local changes and start processes.

dangerous_patterns.title: Local Secret File Handling
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill directs creation of local environment files and recommends gitignore coverage. This pattern is common for Firebase setup, but marketplace users should understand that secret values remain their responsibility.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The lines explicitly mention .env files. The same file also tells users to gitignore them, which lowers the security concern.

content.user_title: Set Up Firebase Projects with Emulators
content.value_statement: New Firebase projects often fail when architecture, emulators, and rules are added late. This skill guides setup decisions, CLI initialization, local testing, and baseline structure.
actual_capabilities: Guides new Firebase project initialization with Firebase CLI.
Collects hosting, authentication, functions, and security model decisions.
Recommends emulator configuration and local verification steps.
Defines TypeScript Cloud Functions structure patterns.
Includes setup steps for tests, linting, git, and environment files.
limitations: It is for new projects, not debugging existing Firebase apps.
It references example documents that are not included in this package.
It does not deploy Firebase resources or provision cloud billing.
It requires Firebase CLI access and user-approved local commands.

use_cases.title: Start a Firebase MVP
use_cases.description: Create a clean project foundation with hosting, functions, rules, tests, and emulators from the first commit.

use_cases.title: Standardize Team Setup
use_cases.description: Use one checklist for project structure, local tooling, and architecture decisions across Firebase repositories.

use_cases.title: Teach Firebase Basics
use_cases.description: Walk a learner through setup choices and explain why each emulator, rules, and testing step matters.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic Firebase App
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to help me initialize a new Firebase project with hosting, Firestore, Functions, and emulators.

prompt_templates.title: Choose Architecture Options
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to ask me the Firebase architecture questions before setup, then summarize the recommended project structure.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare a Team Template
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to design a repeatable Firebase starter workflow for our team, including tests, linting, and local emulator checks.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a Secure Functions Project
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to plan a Firebase project with TypeScript functions, server-write-only Firestore rules, API key tooling, and emulator verification.

output_examples.input: Set up a new Firebase app for a small product.
output: A setup checklist covering Firebase CLI login, project directory creation, Firebase init, and emulator startup.
Architecture decisions for hosting, authentication, functions, and Firestore write strategy.
Verification steps for build, tests, linting, emulator UI, and initial git commit.

output_examples.input: Help our team choose a functions structure.
output: A comparison of Express API, domain-grouped, and individual function layouts.
A recommended layout based on endpoint count, middleware needs, and team ownership.
A follow-up checklist for tests, shared types, and service boundaries.

output_examples.input: Make our Firebase setup safer before we write features.
output: A server-write-first security model with default deny rules.
Environment file guidance that keeps local secrets out of git.
Local emulator checks before the first feature is added.

best_practices: Confirm each command before it changes files or authenticates Firebase CLI.
Start emulators before writing features that depend on Firestore or Functions.
Keep real secret values out of templates, commits, and shared prompts.
anti_patterns: Do not use this skill to patch an existing Firebase setup.
Do not skip architecture questions and accept defaults blindly.
Do not commit local environment files with real credentials.

faq.question: Does this skill create a Firebase project automatically?
faq.answer: It guides the setup workflow. Any CLI command should still be approved by the user.

faq.question: Is it safe for production credentials?
faq.answer: Use placeholders in examples and keep real secrets in local files or managed secret storage.

faq.question: Can it configure Firebase emulators?
faq.answer: Yes. It recommends emulator setup, UI access, and verification steps for local development.

faq.question: Does it support TypeScript Cloud Functions?
faq.answer: Yes. It recommends TypeScript functions and includes structure choices for common Firebase architectures.

faq.question: Can I use it for an existing app?
faq.answer: It is designed for new projects. Existing apps need a review or debugging workflow instead.

faq.question: Why is the security rating medium?
faq.answer: The skill uses legitimate setup commands and environment files, but those actions can affect the local workspace.
